title: Student regrade request with instructor resolution
user_story: As a student, I want to request a regrade for my assignment and provide a reason, so that I can have my work reviewed again if I believe it was graded incorrectly.
rationale: Grades are already released to students but there is no structured dispute flow. Adding a regrade request + instructor resolution queue replaces ad-hoc emails with a traceable, in-product workflow.
machine_acceptance_criteria: Given a student views a released grade, when they submit a regrade request with a non-empty reason, then a regrade_requests row is created with status 'pending'.
Given a regrade request already exists for a submission, when the student tries to submit another, then the form is disabled and shows the existing request status.
Given an instructor views the assessment detail page, when pending regrade requests exist, then those student submission cards appear at the top of the list with a visible badge.

Given an instructor opens a regrade request, when they edit the grade and click Resolve, then the grade is updated, the regrade_requests row status is set to 'resolved', and the student's grade view reflects the new score.
Given an instructor resolves a regrade request, when the student views their grade, then the updated score and a 'Regrade resolved' status are shown.
Given any regrade API route is called without authentication, then it returns 401.
human_acceptance_criteria: Student can open a released submission, fill in a reason, and click 'Request Regrade' — a confirmation message appears and the form is disabled.
Instructor can see pending regrade requests highlighted at the top of the assessment submissions list.
Instructor can open a regrade request, see the student's reason, edit the rubric scores, and click Resolve — the request disappears from the pending list and the grade is updated.
Student refreshes their grade page and sees the resolved score.
implementation_plan: Add regrade_requests table to db/schema.ts (submissionId, studentMembershipId, reason, status enum pending/resolved, resolvedByMembershipId nullable, resolvedAt nullable, createdAt, updatedAt).

Add DB helpers in lib/course-management.ts: createRegradeRequest, getExistingRegradeRequest, listRegradeRequestsForAssignment, resolveRegradeRequest (updates grade + marks resolved in one transaction).
Add PATCH /api/.../submissions/[submissionId]/regrade — instructor resolves request, accepts updated rubric scores in body.
On student grade page (/grade): add a 'Request Regrade' card below Instructor Feedback, hidden if grade not released.
On instructor assessment page: sort and badge submissions that have a pending regrade request, pinning them to the top.
Add a regrade resolution page for instructors: shows student reason, editable rubric scores (reuse SubmissionGradeForm), and a Resolve button.
Add unit tests for POST and PATCH routes, and resolveRegradeRequest DB helper.
backend_changes: db/schema.sql: add CREATE TABLE for regrade_requests.
frontend_changes: app/courses/[courseId]/assessments/[assignmentId]/submissions/[submissionId]/grade/page.tsx: add RegradeRequestCard below Instructor Feedback when grade is released.
New component: _components/regrade-request-card.tsx — textarea + submit button, collapses to status after submission.
app/courses/[courseId]/assessments/[assignmentId]/page.tsx: pin and badge submissions with pending regrade requests at the top of the list.
New page: app/courses/[courseId]/assessments/[assignmentId]/submissions/[submissionId]/regrade/page.tsx — instructor sees student reason, editable grade form, Resolve button.
test_changes: POST regrade route: success, duplicate request, grade not released, unauthenticated.
PATCH regrade route: success, request not found, unauthorized (student cannot resolve), unauthenticated.
resolveRegradeRequest DB helper: updates grade and sets status to resolved atomically.
known_risks_assumptions: Grade must be released (isReleasedToStudent=true) before a regrade can be requested.
One pending regrade request per submission enforced at the application layer.
Resolving overwrites the existing grade row rather than creating a new one — simpler for now, no audit trail of the original grade.
The regrade resolution UI reuses SubmissionGradeForm; if that component changes, the regrade page inherits those changes.
